name: Kythnos
intro: Kythnos is the first Cycladic island you reach from the Athens Riviera, yet it remains one of the least visited. It has no airport, one main ferry link, and a population of just 1,500. The draw: 99 beaches, ancient thermal springs that have drawn bathers for 2,000 years, and an absolutely pristine double-ended beach at Kolona that looks photoshopped.
subtitle: Thermal springs, empty beaches, and the magical Kolona

itinerary.title: 2-day Itinerary for Kythnos
itinerary.subtitle: Thermal springs, empty beaches, and the magical Kolona

stops.name: Merichas (port)
stops.desc: Small ferry port with a horseshoe sandy beach and tavernas on the waterfront.

stops.name: Chora (Messaria)
stops.desc: Main inland village — maze of narrow whitewashed lanes. Walk the main artery end-to-end.

stops.name: Katafyki Cave
stops.desc: Old iron mine turned geological attraction. Short guided tours in summer.

stops.name: Loutra Thermal Baths
stops.desc: Ancient thermal springs (52°C) — bathed in since antiquity, housed in a 19th-century spa hotel. Day passes available.

stops.name: Dinner — Karnagio
stops.desc: Friendly taverna on the Loutra waterfront. Fresh fish straight from the pier.

stops.name: Kolona Beach
stops.desc: The famous double beach — a sand spit connecting the main island to the tiny Agios Loukas islet. Swim in two sides at once. Arrive early to beat the crowds.

stops.name: Apokrousi Beach
stops.desc: Larger sandy bay adjacent to Kolona. Usually less crowded, still gorgeous.

stops.name: Dryopida
stops.desc: Red-tiled roofs (unusual in the Cyclades) and a tiny folk museum. Lunch at one of the two village tavernas.

beaches.name: Kolona
beaches.facilities: One canteen in summer, no umbrellas
beaches.desc: Kythnos' icon beach. A narrow white sandbar connecting the island to a small rocky islet — you can swim simultaneously on two different sides.

beaches.name: Apokrousi
beaches.desc: Longer sandy beach right next to Kolona. More space, still very blue water.

beaches.name: Episkopi
beaches.desc: Long sandy south coast beach. Local favorite, calm water, great for children.
